commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The protected maintainer label plus after-only performance proof make this a maintainer judgment item rather than an automated repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes agent runtime branching and a colocated test; it does not alter dependencies, secrets, workflows, packaging, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=2bb7a5a4110319bdbc81994a40b212e44f50f82e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch gates embedded runtime tool construction on `toolsEnabled` and adds a regression test for `compat.supportsTools: false` models skipping `createOpenClawCodingTools`.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +15. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main computes `toolsEnabled` but does not use it in `shouldConstructTools`, so default no-tool model runs can still build runtime tools before dropping them. I did not run a live model turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected maintainer label plus after-only performance proof make this a maintainer judgment item rather than an automated repair.

target.title: perf(agents): skip tool prep for toolless models
target.body:
## Summary

- skip OpenClaw runtime tool construction when the selected model declares `compat.supportsTools: false`
- add regression coverage so no-tool small-model runs do not pay the tool factory/import cost before the runner drops tools from the prompt

## Verification

- `/usr/bin/time -l node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 24/24 tests passed across 2 shards in 10.20s; max RSS 655081472 bytes
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_9be8e081b61f`, slug `amber-shrimp`, run `run_e8040d0d12c2`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Small/no-tool local models that set `compat.supportsTools: false` no longer build the full OpenClaw runtime tool surface before the runner drops tools from the model prompt.

Real environment tested: Local linked OpenClaw worktree plus AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `/usr/bin/time -l node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: The focused regression/helper tests passed 24/24 across 2 Vitest shards, and AWS Crabbox `run_e8040d0d12c2` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0 across `core` and `coreTests` lanes.

Observed result after fix: The regression asserts `createOpenClawCodingTools` is not called when `supportsModelTools` returns false, while normal tool-enabled runs still construct tools.

What was not tested: A live Ollama/API small-model agent turn was not run in this PR; the follow-up small-model live matrix should cover Ollama plus representative small hosted model families.
target.author: vincentkoc
